exemplar.title: Which images and techniques does Anthony Burgess use to discuss the notion of free will?

detailedExemplarData.title: Which images and techniques does Anthony Burgess use to discuss the notion of free will?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, narrow and consistently linked to analysis chapters
Effective thematic coherenceâeach section returns to the issue of free will
Conclusion synthesizes main analyses and reinforces focus
weaknesses: Methodology relies on some sources of uncertain academic weight
In-text referencing is sporadic and sometimes incomplete
Occasional digressions (e.g. Cold-War speculation) weaken laser focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates accurate and effective topic communication and a clear, focused research question (bands 5â6), but the methodology is not consistently rigorous. On balance, the performance aligns with the middle of the 3â4 band, meriting 3 marks.

strengths: Solid command of the novelâs plot and structure, with purposeful use of key scenes
Generally accurate use of literary and linguistic terminology (e.g. âhyperbolically brutalâ, morphological analysis of Nadsat)
Secondary sources are integrated to extend interpretation rather than merely decorate it
weaknesses: Occasional imprecise vocabulary (e.g. conflating âstructureâ with âsettingâ)
Some online sources lack precise locators (page/section markers)
Opening summary relies heavily on narration without engaging secondary criticism
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge of topic and sources is coherent and effective (upper 3â4 band), though terminology use is mostly accurate rather than fully consistent. Overall performance sits at the top of band 3â4, warranting 3 marks.

strengths: Good variety of sources with general relevance to the research question
Several well-supported analytical insights (e.g. the oxymoronic title symbolism)
Critical evaluation of textual variants (original vs. American editions) shows budding evaluative skill
weaknesses: Several analytical leaps (e.g. Cold-War motives) lack textual evidence
Counter-arguments are only briefly acknowledged and not fully explored
Conclusions often re-state points rather than interrogate them critically
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality and analysis move beyond mere summary but remain inconsistently supported, and evaluation is only partially critical. This aligns with the lower end of the 4â6 band, justifying 2 marks out of 12.

strengths: Clearly appropriate overall structure with all required sections and logical progression
Table of contents and chapter headings enhance readability
weaknesses: Page numbers are absent in the supplied copy
In-text citation style is uneven and URLs break across lines
Minor punctuation and formatting inconsistencies (e.g. dash usage)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs structure fully meets expectations (band 3â4), but uneven layout conventions and missing pagination place it at the lower end of that band, resulting in 2 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay outlines a clear literaryâanalytical method (close reading of four chosen techniques supported by critical commentary) and cites a primary text plus 16 secondary sources.  Selection shows some discernmentâpeer-reviewed journal material and Burgessâs own essays are usedâbut several sources are popular websites or archived television transcripts of uncertain academic weight.  In-text referencing is sporadic; only a handful of citations are integrated into the argument.  Overall the methodology is âmostly completeâ but not consistently rigorous, aligning with the 3-4 band.

Formatting elements (word count, title, headings, sub-headings, reference list) are present and largely consistent.  However, page numbers are not apparent in the supplied copy, in-text citation style is uneven, and several URL breaks suggest imperfect adherence to a formal style guide.  Most but not all layout conventions are met, justifying a score at the lower edge of the 3-4 band.

The student draws on a reasonable variety of sourcesâprimary text, scholarly articles, philosophical works, interviews, and popular criticismâthat are largely relevant to the RQ.  Although a few sources are of middling academic reliability, the majority contribute meaningfully to the argument, satisfying âgood research mostly relevantâ (mid-band 4-6).

The student states a precise literary focus â the images and techniques Burgess employs to explore free will â in the title block and reiterates this purpose in the introduction and conclusion.  Throughout the paper each main section (imagery, structure, Ludovico technique, Nadsat) is explicitly linked back to the overarching issue of free will, showing clear thematic coherence.  Minor lapses in phrasing and some digressions are present, yet the topic remains consistently identifiable and purposive, meeting the descriptor of accurate and effective communication in the 5-6 band.

An argument thread is maintained from introduction through conclusion, and the student comments on differing editions and ethical implications, showing some evaluation.  Still, counter-arguments are only briefly acknowledged, and conclusions often re-state points rather than interrogate them critically.  The discussion therefore fits the descriptor âadequate argument with partially critical evaluationâ, positioning it toward the lower end of the 4-6 band.

Analytical sections move beyond summary by interpreting how each literary device shapes the readerâs stance on free will, and examples from the text are usually quoted or paraphrased to justify claims.  Nonetheless, some leaps in logic (e.g., speculative Cold-War motives) are insufficiently evidenced, and paragraphing occasionally drifts into explanation rather than deeper critique.  Thus the analysis is âgoodâ and mostly supported, meriting the middle of the 4-6 range.

The essay contains all required EE sectionsâtitle page information, table of contents, numbered headingsâand progression is logical.  Transitions between major chapters are clear and the piece stays within word limit.  Hence structure is clearly appropriate, fulfilling the 3-4 band expectations.

The student demonstrates a solid command of the novelâs plot, structure, and thematic motifs, accurately recounting key scenes and their sequencing.  Secondary critics (Evans, Burgessâs own essays, linguistic studies of Nadsat) are used to extend interpretation rather than merely decorate it, indicating coherent understanding.  While the breadth of scholarship is not extensive, the material that is invoked is handled purposefully, so the work reaches the lower end of the âexcellentâ 5-6 band.

Literary terms such as âimageryâ, âcontrastâ, âcyclical structureâ, âargotâ, and philosophical labels (Kantian autonomy, Pavlovian conditioning) are generally applied correctly and consistently.  A few instances of imprecise vocabulary (e.g., equating âstructureâ with âsettingâ) and occasional awkward phrasing lower precision.  On balance, terminology use is mostly accurate but not seamless, placing the work comfortably in the 3-4 band.

The research question is explicitly worded, narrow enough to be manageable, and wholly appropriate to English A: Lang & Lit.  Each analytical chapter is structured around addressing which images or techniques Burgess uses and how they relate to free will, showing a strong operational connection between RQ and discussion.  Although occasional tangential comments (e.g., Cold-War speculation about Russian slang) appear, the line of inquiry remains largely focused, supporting placement in the upper band.

annotations.comment: The clearly structured table of contents shows logical progression of chapters, aiding readability and organization.
Although a table of contents is present, page numbers are missing, which hinders navigation and fails to meet formal layout conventions.
The inclusion of the investigation title at the start clearly orients the reader to the essayâs focus, fulfilling the requirement for a stated title.
The research question is concisely framed and directly addresses the notion of free will, underpinning a focused investigation.
The student correctly provides a word count under 4000, demonstrating compliance with the formatting requirements.
Comparison of original and American editions shows critical evaluation of textual variants and their impact on meaning.
The description of Pavlovian conditioning is precise and effectively linked to Burgessâs method, demonstrating strong conceptual understanding.
The opening summary of the novel demonstrates accurate knowledge of plot and characters but relies solely on narration without engaging secondary sources.
The student restates the research question and focus in the introductionâs closing, reinforcing thematic clarity and purpose.
Key contextual themes such as Cold-War slang speculation are introduced without textual evidence; consider integrating citations for stronger support.
Quoting Evans (1) demonstrates use of secondary criticism, but citation style is inconsistent; unify in-text referencing format.
The term âhyperbolically brutalâ is used accurately, showing an effective application of literary terminology to describe imagery.
The conclusion of Section 2 effectively links imagery and perspective to reader response, illustrating a supported analytical stance.
The dash before âthe issueâ is formatted incorrectly; ensure punctuation conforms to formal academic style.
The link between the 21-chapter structure and the forbidden-fruit effect is insightful; consider citing narrative passages to strengthen the argument.
The student demonstrates effective source selection by integrating FitzGibbon et al. (3) to support the psychological parallel.
The argument on maturation via Pete is convincing, but the section could be more concise to enhance clarity and focus.
While Kantian theory is introduced well, specific textual examples of Alexâs nausea response to Ludovico stimuli would deepen the analysis.
The discussion of the titleâs oxymoron showcases original insight into thematic symbolism and authorial intention.
Several secondary online sources lack page or section indicators; consider adding precise locators to bolster academic rigor.
The morphological analysis of Nadsat exhibits precise linguistic terminology, enriching the discussion of language and freedom.
The link between Nadsat as artificial language change and the Ludovico technique is compelling; adding textual examples of novel passages would further support this claim.
The student critically evaluates the hypothesis of Nadsatâs political connotation by referencing Burgessâs denial, showcasing balanced perspective.
The translatorâs interpretation by Boshnyak adds depth but feels tangential; focus more tightly on evidence directly related to your RQ.
The conclusion synthesizes the main analyses effectively, reinforcing how each technique contributes to the theme of free will.
Ending with an open question invites further reflection; consider linking this to broader implications or potential future research.
The reference list shows breadth of sources, but several URLs break across lines without proper formatting; apply consistent reference styling.
